name: Cabezo de la Mata from Fuente de los Frailes — circular through the Moncayo Natural Park
name_original: Cabezo de la Mata desde la Fuente de los Frailes — circular por el Parque Natural del Moncayo
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails in Moncayo are found within a region characterized by significant altitude variation, from glacial remnants at higher elevations to lush valleys. The landscape features diverse forest ecosystems, including extensive beech, holm oak, and pine forests. Small rivers and ravines wind through the terrain, creating picturesque scenery and opportunities for waterfall hikes. Moncayo Peak, the highest point in the Iberian System, offers panoramic views and contributes to the rugged beauty of the area. Best waterfall hiking trails in Moncayo The most popular waterfall hiking route is Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda (PR-SO 20 y 20.1 Senda Barranco del Val), a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete, leading to a notable waterfall and offering views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña del Tío Fresquito loop from Santa Lucía, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route winds through varied forest ecosystems and offers panoramic views of the Moncayo massif. Local hikers also love the Barranco del Val Trail, a 8.2 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through ravines and diverse forests, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Hiking in Moncayo is defined by diverse forest ecosystems, significant altitude variations, and winding rivers and ravines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Moncayo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Moncayo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are there in Moncayo?
A: Moncayo offers a selection of dedicated waterfall hiking trails. While the region is rich in natural beauty, the guide highlights specific routes that feature waterfalls, such as the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057585100/cascada-del-pozo-de-las-truchas-y-pena-del-tio-fresquito-circular-desde-agreda-pr-so-20-y-20-1-senda-barranco-del-val" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there easy waterfall hikes suitable for beginners in Moncayo?
A: Yes, Moncayo provides options for various skill levels. For an easier experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550735" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito loop from Santa Lucía&lt;/a&gt;. This 4.3-mile (6.9 km) path is rated as easy and winds through varied forest ecosystems, offering panoramic views without significant challenge.

Q: What kind of natural features and attractions can I expect to see on waterfall hikes in Moncayo?
A: Waterfall hikes in Moncayo often lead through diverse landscapes. You'll encounter lush valleys, extensive beech and holm oak forests, and winding rivers and ravines. The region's significant altitude variation means you might also see remnants of past glaciation at higher elevations. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; in the distance, and natural springs like Fuente de la Jarra and Fuente de San Gaudioso along some trails.

Q: When is the best time of year to experience the waterfalls in Moncayo?
A: The best time to experience the waterfalls in Moncayo is typically during spring or after periods of significant rainfall, when the rivers and ravines are fuller, enhancing the cascades. Autumn also offers beautiful scenery with the changing colors of the diverse forest ecosystems, including extensive beech and oak forests.

Q: Are there circular waterfall routes in Moncayo?
A: Yes, there are circular options available. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057585100/cascada-del-pozo-de-las-truchas-y-pena-del-tio-fresquito-circular-desde-agreda-pr-so-20-y-20-1-senda-barranco-del-val" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.9-mile (12.7 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 30 minutes to complete.

Q: What do other hikers say about the waterfall trails in Moncayo?
A: The trails in Moncayo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse forest ecosystems, the picturesque scenery of winding rivers and ravines, and the well-marked paths that make exploration straightforward.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while hiking near waterfalls in Moncayo?
A: Moncayo Natural Park is known for its abundant fauna. While hiking near waterfalls, you might spot over 100 bird species, including Golden eagles, as well as rabbits, partridges, foxes, wild boars, badgers, and roe deer. The rich biodiversity adds another layer to the outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest near the waterfall trails?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, the Moncayo region offers cultural points of interest. You might find traditional constructions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5249057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Snow Well of Prado de Santa Lucía&lt;/a&gt;, which once stored snow. The nearby town of Tarazona also features significant Mudéjar heritage and a Gothic Cathedral, offering a glimpse into local history.

Q: Are there any places to eat or rest along the waterfall trails?
A: Yes, some trails offer convenient spots for rest and refreshment. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Moncayo Restaurant and Hostel&lt;/a&gt; is located on a trail, providing a place to relax and eat after a hike.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of waterfall hikes in Moncayo?
A: The waterfall hikes in Moncayo range from easy to moderate. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550735" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito loop from Santa Lucía&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) path, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057585100/cascada-del-pozo-de-las-truchas-y-pena-del-tio-fresquito-circular-desde-agreda-pr-so-20-y-20-1-senda-barranco-del-val" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.9-mile (12.7 km) trail.

Q: Where can I find parking for waterfall hikes in Moncayo?
A: Many routes in Moncayo, including those leading to waterfalls, often start from villages like San Martín de la Virgen del Moncayo or Ágreda, which typically offer convenient access points and parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific route details for the most precise parking information.
tips:
Cabezo de la Mata is a peak located in the Sierra del Moncayo, in Aragon, Spain. With an altitude of 1,437 metres, it is one of the most accessible peaks in the area.

One of the most popular routes to ascend Cabezo de la Mata begins at Fuente de los Frailes. This circular route offers spectacular views of the northeastern slope of the Sierra del Moncayo and the plain of the Ebro Valley. The route includes sections through beech and pine forests, and is suitable for all year round.
Hermitage of San Gaudioso. Circus of San Gaudioso. Moncayo Natural Park.
Underground deposit in which snow was stored. El Moncayo was a great snow producing and exporting center.

waypoints.name: Snow Well of Prado de Santa Lucía
